incidents.victim: Aqua Security, Checkmarx, LiteLLM, Telnyx (software supply chain)
incidents.vector: Compromised GitHub Actions and PyPI packages via stolen maintainer credentials, deploying credential-harvesting malware through trusted open-source tooling including Trivy, KICS, LiteLLM, and Telnyx SDK.
incidents.impact: Estimated 300 GB of credentials stolen from hundreds of CI/CD pipelines; 100+ million monthly downloads of affected packages exposed. CISA added CVE-2026-33634 to KEV catalog with April 8 remediation deadline. Group partnered with Vect ransomware-as-a-service and distributed affiliate keys to ~300,000 BreachForums users.

incidents.victim: iOS device users (Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Malaysia, Ukraine)
incidents.attacker: Multiple state-linked espionage groups and commercial surveillance vendors
incidents.vector: Full-chain iOS exploit (DarkSword) leveraging six zero-day vulnerabilities across iOS 18.4-18.7 to deploy GHOSTBLADE, GHOSTKNIFE, and GHOSTSABER surveillance malware families.
incidents.impact: Victims in at least four countries subjected to full device compromise including message interception, location tracking, and microphone/camera access. Apple patched in iOS 26.3. Multiple state actors including suspected Russian espionage operators adopted the exploit chain.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of multiple zero-day vulnerabilities in Cisco SD-WAN systems that went undetected for over three years; five of nine disclosed vulnerabilities confirmed actively exploited in the wild.
incidents.impact: Network management and control-plane infrastructure compromised at enterprise scale. CISA added two vulnerabilities to KEV catalog. Attackers maintained persistent access to network edge infrastructure across multiple sectors.

incidents.victim: Organizations in education, healthcare, engineering, and government
incidents.vector: Exploited critical zero-day in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center (CVE-2026-20131, CVSS 10) for arbitrary Java code execution weeks before public disclosure on March 4.
incidents.impact: Attackers had zero-day access from January 26, deploying custom malware, reconnaissance scripts, and webshells across multiple victim networks. Previous Interlock targets include St. Paul, Minnesota government and DaVita dialysis company.

incidents.vector: Years-long cyber espionage campaign (tracked as CL-STA-1087) using novel backdoors and credential-stealing tools, with initial access via suspicious PowerShell activity and persistent footholds maintained across military networks.
incidents.impact: Strategic military intelligence collected over multiple years from Southeast Asian defense organizations. Additional overlapping campaigns (CL-STA-1048, CL-STA-1049) deployed multiple RAT families against the same government targets.

incidents.victim: Hundreds of organizations (construction, law, nonprofits, real estate, manufacturing, healthcare)
incidents.vector: AI-generated phishing emails exploiting Microsoft device authentication OAuth flow via Railway platform infrastructure; unique lures bypass email security filters at scale.
incidents.impact: 344+ confirmed victims with 50+ new compromises per day. Attackers obtain 90-day OAuth tokens bypassing MFA entirely. Thousands likely affected enterprise-wide across multiple sectors.

incidents.vector: Compromised personal email account of FBI Director; leaked personal photographs, emails from 2010-2019, and documents via newly created websites after FBI seized four prior domains.
incidents.impact: Historical personal email data exposed; FBI confirmed no government information compromised. US government offered $10 million bounty on group members. Group claimed retaliation for FBI domain seizures and the sinking of Iranian Navy frigate IRIS Dena.

incidents.vector: Coordinated phishing campaign using fake automated Signal support notices to trick victims into providing verification codes and account PINs, enabling attacker device linking or full account takeover.
incidents.impact: Thousands of accounts compromised globally including US government officials, military personnel, and journalists. FBI, French, and Dutch intelligence agencies issued security alerts.

incidents.vector: Compromised Microsoft Intune administrative account and used the device management system's native wipe functionality to remotely wipe over 200,000 company devices globally.
incidents.impact: Hospital operations disrupted in Maryland and nationwide; some surgeries cancelled due to unavailable implants. Production lines offline for approximately two weeks. Operations affected across US, Ireland, and India. FBI/CISA issued joint advisory on March 18.

incidents.victim: Diplomatic organizations and critical infrastructure (energy, maritime, finance) in the Middle East
incidents.vector: Expanded cyberespionage campaign using new Rust-based malware (LampoRAT, BlackBeard) with UDP-based backdoors and AI-assisted development techniques targeting energy, maritime, and finance sectors.
incidents.impact: Critical infrastructure across the Middle East under active surveillance. Campaign represents a significant capability upgrade with new tooling and expanded targeting scope amid ongoing Iran military conflict.

incidents.vector: Active exploitation of CVE-2026-33017 (CVSS 9.8) — unauthenticated code injection in Langflow API endpoint allowing arbitrary Python execution via exec() without sandboxing.
incidents.impact: 1,000+ exploitation attempts across multiple regions within 24 hours of disclosure; attackers deploying info stealers, reverse shells, and cryptominers. API keys for OpenAI, Anthropic, and AWS extracted. CISA added to KEV catalog March 25; remediated in Langflow 1.9.0.

incidents.vector: Published malicious PyPI package versions (4.87.1, 4.87.2) using stolen credentials; novel steganography technique embedding payloads in WAV audio files to blend with Telnyx voice/telecom API context.
incidents.impact: 670,000+ monthly downloads affected; platform-specific credential harvesters deployed on Windows, Linux, and macOS. PyPI quarantined both versions within hours. Last known safe version: 4.87.0.

incidents.vector: Attackers claimed to have used credentials stolen from the TeamPCP supply chain campaign to breach internal systems; offering access for sale via encrypted messaging.
incidents.impact: Claimed 3 GB breach including internal code repositories, AWS/Azure configurations, GitHub Enterprise user data, and employee information. Breach unconfirmed — AstraZeneca has not issued a public statement.

incidents.victim: Ubiquiti UniFi Networking Application users (88,000+ exposed instances)
incidents.vector: Maximum-severity path-traversal vulnerability (CVE-2026-22557, CVSS 10) in UniFi Network Application allowing unauthenticated account takeover and privilege escalation.
incidents.impact: 88,000+ instances publicly exposed on the internet, one-third in the United States. Exploitation is trivial to automate. No confirmed active exploitation yet but expected imminently given severity and exposure.

incidents.vector: Ransomware deployed via compromised administrative account with device wiper functionality rather than traditional encryption; attackers cleared logs and traces.
incidents.impact: Healthcare operations disrupted; no data exfiltration detected. Unusual destructive approach suggests strategic motivations beyond financial gain amid Iran military conflict escalation. Pay2Key has accumulated 170 victims and $8M in ransoms since summer 2025.

incidents.vector: Self-propagating worm (CanisterWorm) spreading through exposed Docker APIs, Kubernetes clusters, and Redis servers; executes data destruction on systems matching Iran timezone or Farsi language settings.
incidents.impact: Wiper destroys data on all nodes in compromised Kubernetes clusters; uses Internet Computer Protocol canisters for command and control. Shares infrastructure with the broader TeamPCP supply chain campaign.

incidents.victim: Iranian dissidents and journalists in the United States and abroad
incidents.vector: Custom malware masquerading as legitimate applications (Pictory, KeePass, Telegram) delivered via social engineering on social media platforms; uses Telegram as C2 infrastructure.
incidents.impact: Full remote access to victim devices including screen capture, audio recording, file exfiltration, and location tracking. FBI issued alert on March 24. Death threats and $250,000 bounty offers sent to targets via associated email accounts.

incidents.victim: Ransomware victims across multiple US companies
incidents.vector: Aleksei Volkov (26, St. Petersburg) operated as an initial access broker — identified vulnerabilities, gained unauthorized access to corporate networks, and sold access to ransomware groups including Yanluowang.
incidents.impact: Volkov sentenced to 81 months in federal prison on March 23. Facilitated dozens of ransomware attacks causing $9 million in actual losses and $24 million in intended losses.

incidents.victim: Israeli Defense Force personnel, Sanzer Hasidic Jewish community
incidents.vector: Stole PII and sensitive data on ~190 IDF-associated individuals and 851 GB of confidential data from Sanzer community members; posted data with threatening messages on Handala Hack domains.
incidents.impact: Personal information, financial documents, and private correspondence exposed with explicit threats including location/residence details. FBI seized both primary domains on March 19; group declared plans to continue operations from new infrastructure.

incidents.vector: Breach of European Commission AWS cloud account; method of initial access not publicly disclosed.
incidents.impact: Data stolen from European Commission cloud infrastructure. Investigation ongoing with limited details publicly available.
